evals.prompt: Parse this data table file and extract column information: 'data/NearbyGalaxies_Jan2021_PUBLIC.fits'
evals.expected_output: A markdown table with 49 columns showing name, description, units, and type. Descriptions should be populated for most columns (sourced from FITS TCOMMn headers or embedded VOTable XML). Units are not stored in the file but should be inferred from column names and descriptions (e.g. velocity columns → km/s, magnitudes → mag, proper motions → mas/yr, ellipticity → dimensionless_unscaled).

assertions.text: Output contains a markdown table with Column, Description, Units, and Type headers
assertions.evidence: Table written to NearbyGalaxies_Jan2021_PUBLIC-parsed-data-table.md with | Column | Description | Units | Type | headers

assertions.text: Output contains all 49 column names from the FITS file
assertions.evidence: All 49 columns present, confirmed by TFIELDS=49 in FITS header

assertions.text: Descriptions are populated for key columns like GalaxyName, RA, Dec, EB-V, dmod, vh, Vmag (not all '—')
assertions.evidence: Descriptions sourced from embedded VOTable XML (VOTMETA=T); uncertainty columns inferred from base column; sigma_s- misplacement noted and corrected

assertions.text: Unit 'dimensionless_unscaled' is assigned to the ellipticity column (e=1-b/a) via units inference
assertions.evidence: units-inference.md rule 'dimensionless quantities (e.g. ellipticity, ratios) → dimensionless_unscaled' applied to e=1-b/a, e+, e-

assertions.text: In Step 0, the agent checks for `astrodb-build-artifacts/directions.md` (not `artifacts/directions.md`) and reads it if present

evals.prompt: Parse this data table file and extract column information: 'data/arecibo_radar_observed_objects_list.csv'
evals.expected_output: A markdown table with 8 columns showing name, description, units, and type. Since this is a plain CSV with no embedded metadata, descriptions should be inferred from column names and sample data. The 'Absolute Magnitute' column should have unit 'mag'. The typo in the column name should be noted.

assertions.text: Output contains a markdown table with Column, Description, Units, and Type headers
assertions.evidence: Table written to /tmp/arecibo-columns.md with | Column | Description | Units | Type | headers

assertions.text: Output contains all 8 column names from the CSV file
assertions.evidence: All 8 columns present: Number, Name, Designation, Category, Components, Absolute Magnitute, Quality Code for Best Images, NASA Link

assertions.text: Descriptions are inferred for all columns (no rows with only '—' as description)
assertions.evidence: All 8 columns have inferred descriptions from column names and sample data

assertions.text: Unit 'mag' is assigned to the 'Absolute Magnitute' column via units inference
assertions.evidence: units-inference.md rule 'magnitude → mag' applied; unit set to 'mag'

assertions.text: The typo in 'Absolute Magnitute' (should be 'Magnitude') is noted
assertions.evidence: Note included in both markdown and HTML output: 'Absolute Magnitute contains a typo in the source file — should be Absolute Magnitude'

assertions.text: astropy was used as the reader
assertions.evidence: astropy check passed on first try; Table.read() used; sidecar written with reader='astropy'

evals.prompt: Parse this data table file and extract column information: 'data/MRT-velo.txt'
evals.expected_output: A markdown table with 14 columns (ID, VLSR, e_VLSR, SType, VLSR_G, VLSR_S, VLSR_F, VLSR_T, VLSR_DHT, VLSR_DGT, DGT, VLSR_C, VLSR_M, e_VLSR_M). This is a CDS/AAS machine-readable table (MRT) with an embedded byte-by-byte description header, so descriptions and units (mostly km/s) should come directly from that header rather than being inferred or left as '—'. The reader must recognize the file isn't plain CSV and use astropy's ascii.mrt format. Several velocity columns (e.g. VLSR_G, VLSR_S, VLSR_F, VLSR_T) use the literal value 999 as a fill value for missing data in most rows — this should be flagged as a sentinel/placeholder in the notes, not reported as a real measurement.

assertions.text: File was read successfully as a table (not misparsed as a single garbage CSV column)
All 14 columns are present in the output
Descriptions are populated from the embedded byte-by-byte header (not left as '—')
Units are populated as km/s for velocity columns
The 999 sentinel/fill-value pattern is flagged in the notes rather than reported as real data
astropy was used as the reader (not a pandas fallback)

evals.prompt: Parse this data table file and extract column information: 'data/MRT-hcsc.txt'
evals.expected_output: A markdown table with 17 columns (ID, DIAM, e_DIAM, MASS, e_MASS, BLUM, e_BLUM, LMRAT, e_LMRAT, TEMP, e_TEMP, LRAT, e_LRAT, TBOL, e_TBOL, SIGMA, e_SIGMA). This is also a CDS/MRT table, so descriptions and units should come from the embedded byte-by-byte header (e.g. pc, solMass/Msun, Lsun, K, g/cm^2). The SIGMA/e_SIGMA unit 'g/cm^2' triggers an astropy UnrecognizedUnit warning when read — the unit should still be reported as 'g/cm^2' rather than dropped or left blank.

assertions.text: File was read successfully as a table (not misparsed as a single garbage CSV column)
All 17 columns are present in the output
Descriptions are populated from the embedded byte-by-byte header (not left as '—')
SIGMA/e_SIGMA units are reported as g/cm^2 despite the UnrecognizedUnit warning
astropy was used as the reader (not a pandas fallback)
